title: Bulk report - Week 8
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market maintained its continued momentum throughout the week, improving $2,277 week-on week to close at $8,216, which is the highest value since late January. West Australian miners remained relatively inactive despite the reopening of West Australian ports and vessels returning to berths. The C5 route gradually strengthened, reaching $6.485 by week's end, with the laycan window now fully shifted to March dates.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market maintained its continued momentum throughout the week, improving $2,277 week-on week to close at $8,216, which is the highest value since late January. West Australian miners remained relatively inactive despite the reopening of West Australian ports and vessels returning to berths.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The Panamax market had another an eventful week, with the market trend finding some strength in both arenas as strong fundamentals came to the fore. The week began sedately, only to spark into life mid-week with an FFA drive, only to level off as the week ended.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Overall, a more positive week for the sector, led by the increased demand seen in Asia. The Atlantic started on a positive note, although as the week progressed demand from the US Gulf tapered off slightly and there was an easing of rates, with a 61,000-dwt fixing from here for a trip to Chittagong at $17,500.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: A positive week in general with rates across most loading areas were showing increases compared to previous levels. The Continent-Mediterranean region continued to see gradual improvement throughout the week, with sentiment remaining largely positional.
